---
title: "Applied Python"
subtitle: "Methods for Data Science pathway"
---

## Course Overview

Applied Python introduces core programming and data analysis workflows for early-career researchers. The course builds from Python fundamentals through array operations, visualization, modelling, and robust coding practices.

## Who This Is For

- Researchers with little or no prior Python experience.
- Learners transitioning from another language to Python for data science.
- Research staff who need reproducible, maintainable analysis workflows.

## What You Need

- A laptop with Python 3.8+ installed.
- Access to lesson datasets and example code.
- A Python environment such as Jupyter Notebook, VS Code, or IPython.

## Lesson Sequence

| Lesson | Focus |
|---|---|
| [01 Types, Variables, and Basic Operations](episodes/01-types_variables_and_basic_operations.qmd) | Core Python types, variables, lists, slicing |
| [02 Loops and Control Flow](episodes/02-loops_and_control_flow.qmd) | Iteration, conditionals, boolean logic |
| [03 Data Manipulation and Visualization](episodes/03-data_manipulation_and_visualization.qmd) | NumPy arrays, plotting, multi-file workflows |
| [04 Functions](episodes/04_functions.qmd) | Defining, testing, and documenting functions |
| [05 Data Manipulation with pandas](episodes/05-data_manipulation_with_pandas.qmd) | DataFrames, filtering, cleaning, grouped summaries |
| [06 Regression Analysis and Modelling](episodes/06-regression_analysis_and_modelling.qmd) | Linear regression with scikit-learn |
| [07 Python Object-Oriented Programming](episodes/07-python_object_oriented_programming.qmd) | Classes, methods, inheritance, design choices |
| [08 Errors, Debugging, and Defensive Programming](episodes/08-errors_debugging_and_defensive_programming.qmd) | Tracebacks, assertions, testing, debugging workflow |

## Course Materials

### Learners

- [Setup](learners/setup.qmd)
- [Reference](learners/reference.qmd)
- [Discussion](learners/discuss.qmd)

### Instructors

- [Instructor Notes](instructors/instructor-notes.qmd)
- [Additional Material](instructors/additional_material.qmd)
- [Extra Exercises](instructors/extra_exercises.qmd)

### Community and Profiles

- [Course README](README.qmd)
- [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.qmd)
- [Learner Profiles](profiles/learner-profiles.qmd)
- [Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.qmd)
- [License](LICENSE.qmd)
- [Authors](AUTHORS.qmd)

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
### Attribution
These lessons are derived from materials developed by the [Software Carpentry](https://software-carpentry.org) project.

The original content is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) license: [https://github.com/swcarpentry/python-novice-inflammation/blob/main/LICENSE.md](https://github.com/swcarpentry/python-novice-inflammation/blob/main/LICENSE.md)
:::
